pi_items.source: Fictional designation for a task set in March 2026. Intentionally synthetic — no real transient with this name exists. Consistent across task.json, setup_task.sh alert document, and verifier.py ATel content check.

Computed from sexagesimal RA 12h 33m 52.0s in alert document: 12 + 33/60 + 52/3600 = 12.56444, rounds to 12.5644. Consistent with alert text, task.json, and verifier RA pattern check ('12.5' substring).
Computed from sexagesimal Dec +07d 41' 48.0" in alert document: 7 + 41/60 + 48/3600 = 7.69667, rounds to 7.6967. Consistent across all task files.

NGC 4526 is a real SAB(s)0 lenticular galaxy in the Virgo Cluster, confirmed via SIMBAD (simbad.u-strasbg.fr). It hosted SN 1994D (Type Ia) and SN 1969E. The OpenNGC.kscat catalog in the KStars installation includes it (ShowNGC=true in kstarsrc). Live-tested: telescope successfully slewed to NGC 4526 coordinates.

Computed from alert RA 12h 34m 03.0s: 12 + 34/60 + 3/3600 = 12.5675. Cross-checked against SIMBAD NGC 4526 J2000 coordinates: RA 12h 34m 03.029s = 12.56751h. Difference is 0.03 arcsec-of-time — effectively exact match.

Computed from alert Dec +07d 41' 57.0": 7 + 41/60 + 57/3600 = 7.69917, rounds to 7.6992. SIMBAD gives Dec +07 41 56.904 = 7.69914. Difference is 0.10 arcsec — effectively exact match with authoritative SIMBAD coordinates.

SA 104 (Selected Area 104) is a real Landolt UBVRI photometric standard field, confirmed via ESO (eso.org/sci/observing/tools/standards/Landolt.html), Keck Observatory, CFHT, and the AAVSO Standard Fields Database. It appears in both Landolt (1992, AJ 104, 340) and Landolt (2009, AJ 137, 4186). Contains 34 standard stars spanning V ~9.4 to 16.1.

Computed from alert RA 12h 42m 00.0s: 12 + 42/60 = 12.7 exactly. Real SA 104 center is approximately RA 12h 42m 20s (Nordic Optical Telescope) to 12h 43m 10s (AAVSO), spanning RA 12h41 to 12h45. The value 12h 42m 00s is within the field extent and is a valid pointing coordinate for SA 104 observations.

Computed from alert Dec -00d 32' 00.0": -(32/60) = -0.53333. Real SA 104 field center is approximately Dec -00 34' (AAVSO gives -0.566°), spanning Dec -00:51 to -00:17. The value -00d 32' is within the field extent (~34 arcmin range) and is a valid pointing for SA 104 observations. Offset from exact center is ~2 arcmin.

Design parameter for verifier coordinate checking. The current verifier stub does not perform coordinate validation, so this value is not enforced. Cannot be verified against external data — it is a task design choice.

setup_task.sh configures Filter Simulator slot 1 as 'Luminance'. Alert document states 'Luminance (Slot 1)'. Live-tested: indi_getprop confirmed Filter Simulator.FILTER_NAME.FILTER_SLOT_NAME_1=Luminance after setup. Consistent across all files.

setup_task.sh configures: Slot 2=V, Slot 3=B, Slot 4=R on both Filter Simulator and CCD Simulator devices. Alert document states 'B (Slot 3), V (Slot 2), R (Slot 4)'. Verifier checks subdirectories candidate/B, candidate/V, candidate/R. All consistent. Live-tested: confirmed via indi_getprop.
Alert document Phase 1 states 'Exposure: 60 seconds'. Consistent with task.json metadata. Not explicitly checked by verifier (verifier checks file count, not exposure time).
Alert document Phase 2 states 'Exposure: 30 seconds per frame'. Consistent with task.json metadata.
Alert document Phase 3 states 'Exposure: 15 seconds per frame'. Consistent with task.json metadata.
Alert document Phase 1 states '3 frames minimum'. Verifier awards 10 pts for count_new_fits('reference') >= 3. Consistent.
Alert document Phase 2 states '5 frames per filter (minimum)'. Verifier awards 10 pts per filter for count >= 5. Consistent.
Alert document Phase 3 states '3 frames per filter (minimum)'. Verifier awards 5 pts per filter for count >= 3. Consistent.

setup_task.sh creates this directory tree (reference/, candidate/{B,V,R}, standard/{B,V,R}, charts/). export_result.sh scans it via os.walk(). Verifier checks subdirectories within it. Live-tested: confirmed directory exists with correct structure and ga:ga ownership.

Alert document Phase 4 states 'Write a Python script at /home/ga/reduce_photometry.py'. export_result.sh checks this exact path. Verifier checks script_info.exists and decodes content_b64. Live-tested: confirmed ga user can write to this path.

Alert document Phase 4 states 'Writes output to /home/ga/classification.json'. export_result.sh reads this file with cat. Verifier checks class_info.exists and parses JSON content for 6 required keys. Live-tested: confirmed ga user can write to this path.

Alert document Phase 5 states 'Write /home/ga/Documents/atel_draft.txt'. export_result.sh checks this path and base64-encodes first 80 lines. Verifier checks existence, mtime, and content keywords. Live-tested: confirmed ga user can write to this path.

setup_task.sh creates this file via heredoc. Live-tested: confirmed file exists (5748 bytes), readable by ga user, contains all 5 phases with correct coordinates, filter slots, exposure times, directories, standard magnitudes, reduction formula, and classification criteria.

These are SYNTHETIC standard magnitudes defined for this task. They do NOT correspond to any specific real star in SA 104. Real SA 104 primary standards include: 104-461 (V=9.71, B=10.18, R=9.42), 104-306 (V=9.36, B=10.95, R=8.53), 104-335 (V=11.67, B=12.29, R=11.31). The task values (B=11.24, V=10.51, R=10.12) yield B-V=0.73, V-R=0.39, which are plausible for a late F/early G star and are self-consistent. They serve as task input parameters for the agent's reduction script, not as real astronomical measurements. Alert document and task.json metadata are consistent.
Persona string, not verifiable against external data. Transient astronomy is a real subdiscipline.
Persona string, not verifiable against external data.

privileged_info_summary: Task requires classifying fictional supernova candidate AT2026xy near real galaxy NGC 4526 (SIMBAD: RA 12h34m03.03s, Dec +07d41m56.9s, SAB(s)0 lenticular, Virgo Cluster, hosted real SN 1994D Type Ia). Candidate position: RA 12h33m52.0s (12.5644h), Dec +07d41m48.0s (7.6967deg). Standard calibration field SA 104 (real Landolt field, Landolt 1992 AJ 104 340) near RA 12h42m00s, Dec -00d32m00s. Task-defined standard magnitudes: B=11.24, V=10.51, R=10.12 (synthetic, do not match any specific SA 104 star). Filter slots: 1=Luminance, 2=V, 3=B, 4=R (set on both Filter Simulator and CCD Simulator INDI devices). Phase 1: 3x60s Luminance of NGC 4526 to /home/ga/Images/sn_followup/reference/, finding chart via capture_sky_view.sh with cool palette. Phase 2: 5x30s each B/V/R of candidate to candidate/{B,V,R}/. Phase 3: 3x15s each B/V/R of SA 104 to standard/{B,V,R}/. Phase 4: Python script at /home/ga/reduce_photometry.py using astropy, formula m_inst=-2.5*log10(median_ADU), ZP=m_known-m_inst_std, output /home/ga/classification.json with keys candidate_B_mag, candidate_V_mag, candidate_R_mag, B_minus_V, V_minus_R, sn_type_estimate. Classification: B-V<0.2 Type Ia, 0.2-0.8 Type II-P, >0.8 Type Ib/c. Phase 5: ATel draft at /home/ga/Documents/atel_draft.txt. Verifier scores 100pts (15 ref + 30 science + 15 standards + 25 reduction + 15 ATel), pass at 60. Anti-gaming: 3 stale FITS with Jan 2024 mtime seeded in reference/ and candidate/V/; verifier filters by mtime>task_start and size>2048. CCD simulator produces 1280x1024 16-bit FITS ~2.6MB each. Telescope starts at Polaris (RA 2.5h, Dec 89.26deg). astropy 5.0.2 and numpy 1.21.5 available. NGC catalog (OpenNGC.kscat) loaded with ShowNGC=true.
